questions.q: What kind of object was the Sanjeevani?
questions.explanation: The Sanjeevani was a luminous life-restoring medicinal herb.

questions.q: Who was sent to fetch the Sanjeevani herb?
questions.explanation: Hanuman was dispatched to bring the healing herb.

questions.q: For whom was the Sanjeevani herb primarily sought during the war?
questions.explanation: The herb was needed to revive the gravely wounded Lakshmana.

questions.q: On what type of terrain did the Sanjeevani herb grow?
questions.explanation: The herb grew on a far Himalayan peak.

questions.q: When Hanuman could not identify the herb, what did he do?
questions.explanation: Unable to find the specific herb, Hanuman lifted and carried the whole mountain back.

questions.q: In which war did the need for the Sanjeevani arise?
options: The war against Ravana in Lanka
questions.explanation: The herb was sought during Rama's war against Ravana at Lanka.

questions.q: What special ability of Hanuman allowed him to carry the mountain?
questions.explanation: Hanuman's tremendous strength and power of flight let him lift and fly with the mountain.

questions.q: What effect did the Sanjeevani have on the wounded?
questions.explanation: The herb restored life and healed grave battlefield wounds.

questions.q: Hanuman is the son of which wind deity?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is the son of Vayu, the wind god, which aided his flight.

questions.q: The Sanjeevani episode is most famously associated with which book of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The herb-and-mountain episode occurs during the war, narrated in the Yuddha Kanda.

questions.q: Whose advice led to Hanuman being sent for the healing herbs?
questions.explanation: Sushena, the vanara physician, identified the herbs needed and prescribed the remedy.

questions.q: Which mountain is traditionally said to be the one Hanuman carried for the herbs?
questions.explanation: Tradition names Dronagiri, in the Himalayan Gandhamadana region, as the mountain Hanuman bore.

questions.q: Lakshmana fell gravely wounded by a powerful weapon hurled by which warrior?
questions.explanation: Ravana's son Indrajit struck Lakshmana down with a deadly missile, prompting the search for the herb.

questions.q: According to tradition, how many distinct healing herbs did Sushena require from the mountain?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that four great healing herbs were needed, including the Sanjeevani (Mritasanjivani).

questions.q: What made the herbs difficult for Hanuman to identify on the peak?
options: They glowed and looked alike, hiding themselves
questions.explanation: The luminous herbs could not be singled out, so Hanuman took the whole mountain rather than guess wrong.

questions.q: Whom did Hanuman encounter and battle on the way during the herb mission, according to many tellings?
options: Kalanemi, a demon sent by Ravana
questions.explanation: Ravana sent the demon Kalanemi to delay Hanuman, but Hanuman overcame him.

questions.q: Why was speed critical in retrieving the Sanjeevani?
options: The cure had to be applied before daybreak
questions.explanation: The healing had to be completed before sunrise, so Hanuman had to fly with great haste.

questions.q: Who served as the vanara army's healer that applied the herbs?
questions.explanation: The physician Sushena administered the herbs to revive Lakshmana.

questions.q: Hanuman fetched the herbs while serving the army of which exiled monkey king?
questions.explanation: Hanuman served Sugriva, the vanara king allied with Rama.

questions.q: In which mountain range did the Sanjeevani grow?
questions.explanation: The life-restoring herb grew on a peak in the far Himalayas.

questions.q: What is the Sanskrit-derived meaning at the root of the name 'Sanjeevani'?
questions.explanation: Sanjeevani derives from roots meaning to fully revive or restore life.

questions.q: After the herbs healed Lakshmana, what did Hanuman do with the mountain in many traditions?
options: Carried it back and set it in its original place
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Hanuman returned the mountain to its rightful place in the Himalayas.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, which two specific herbs are named among those Hanuman fetched, along with Sanjivani?
questions.explanation: Valmiki names Vishalyakarani (wound-closer) and Savarnyakarani (skin-color-restorer) among the herbs, alongside Sanjivani.

questions.q: The herb Vishalyakarani, fetched with the Sanjeevani, was specifically meant to do what?
options: Draw out weapons and close wounds
questions.explanation: Vishalyakarani's function was to extract embedded weapons and heal the wounds they left.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, on which earlier night does Lakshmana also fall, requiring Hanuman's first herb-mountain journey?
options: When struck by Indrajit's Brahmastra-empowered shakti weapon
questions.explanation: In Valmiki's telling Lakshmana is felled by Indrajit's mighty shakti (dart), prompting the herb mission.

questions.q: Which deity, as Kalanemi's ally or in disguise, is said to have tried to deceive Hanuman during the herb mission in many regional retellings?
options: A false ascetic and an apsara-cursed crocodile sent by Ravana
Yama in the form of a deer
questions.explanation: Kalanemi posed as an ascetic and used a cursed crocodile (a freed apsara) to try to trap Hanuman, who saw through the ruse.

questions.q: According to the broader tradition, who was the original celestial physician credited with knowledge of such revival herbs?
questions.explanation: Dhanvantari, the divine physician of the gods, is traditionally linked with such life-restoring medicine.

questions.q: The mountain from which the herbs came is associated in tradition with which fragrant Himalayan region?
questions.explanation: The herb-bearing peak is tied to the Gandhamadana ('intoxicating fragrance') region of the Himalayas.

questions.q: Trikuta is the name associated in the Ramayana with which location, distinct from the herb mountain?
options: The mountain on which Ravana's Lanka stood
The peak where the herbs grew
The site of the bridge's start
questions.explanation: Trikuta is the mountain on which the city of Lanka was built, not the Himalayan herb peak.

questions.q: In Valmiki's account, after the herbs revived the fallen, what additionally happened to slain vanaras whose bodies had been cast into the sea?
options: They could not be revived, as their bodies were lost
They all rose instantly from the seabed
questions.explanation: Valmiki notes that vanaras whose bodies had been thrown into the ocean could not be revived by the herbs, since they were beyond reach.
